key_insights.insight: Often, escaping a difficult situation can be a powerful motivator for achieving success.
key_insights.quote: It's more sometimes as you described it like an escape from something else like yeah and we don't think of motivation as escapism all the time or insecurity but it so often is.

key_insights.insight: True strength is in vulnerability and being able to ask for help.
key_insights.quote: He made me start seeing that some of it isn't my responsibility... to understand my patterns when I need help and that it's okay to ask for help it's not weak it's not a weakness.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes we spend more time studying other people than ourselves.
key_insights.quote: We spend more time as people studying other people, what you wear, what you study, how you act, how you behave than actually spending time studying ourselves

key_insights.insight: Functional depression can exist even when someone achieves external success, but unresolved emotions will still be carried.
key_insights.quote: I was like a functional depressive this whole time... I wasn't happy. It was heavy. Taking on everyone's emotions... and then I'd be home in the evening and be like, whoa, I don't want to talk to anyone.

key_insights.insight: It's important to resolve conflict in relationships, otherwise resentment and contempt builds up.
key_insights.quote: That's the thing in relationships you've got to battle it out to resolve a problem and if you don't it ends up being deposited in the relationship as resentment or contempt and it just stays there as an issue unaddressed.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes, to move forward in life, you have to address past trauma and pain.
key_insights.quote: In order to move forward or see into the future you can't if you're still driving looking in the rearview mirror like not dealing with all the past stuff behind

key_insights.insight: When someone is constantly trying to help and please others, it often comes at the expense of their own happiness.
key_insights.quote: If you spend your life like trying to help everybody else and make everyone else happy it's coming at the cost of something coming at the cost of your own happiness often and and that is difficult

actionable_advice.advice: Take quiet time for self-reflection to allow thoughts about life to surface and re-energize you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Escape to a quiet, peaceful place, especially in nature, for hiking or other reflective activities.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are feeling emotionally numb, Google for 'therapy' and research providers.
actionable_advice.how_to: Look for someone who will be brutally honest with you, and whom you feel comfortable with.
Instead of rushing on to the next thing, take the time to enjoy and remember what you are doing.
Embrace a mindset of 'face the fear see the fear and face it and do it anyway'.

memorable_stories: Alex's memories of growing up in East London and playing football in the cage.
The story of her mother finally standing up to her father at a birthday party.
The story of Alex receiving a text from her father asking for tickets to Strictly Come Dancing.
The story of two black women shouting "Keep going" to her from a park bench during the pandemic.
contrarian_claims: Motivation is often born out of escapism or insecurity, rather than a deliberate choice.
Often we need to address our experiences after we have left a bad environment.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Alex Scott: I’ve Never Told The FULL Truth About My Past
